examples.sentence: Unlike everyone else in her office, Jenna always seems to march to the beat of a different drum, choosing unconventional methods to solve problems.
examples.translation: Unlike everyone else in her office, Jenna always seems to act in a nonconformist way, choosing unconventional methods to solve problems.

examples.sentence: If you want to succeed in this industry, you can’t just follow the crowd; you have to march to the beat of a different drum.
examples.translation: If you want to succeed in this industry, you need to behave in a unique, nonconformist way rather than follow the crowd.

examples.sentence: Tom marched to the beat of a different drum, refusing to wear the standard uniform and always expressing his own style.
examples.translation: Tom behaved in a nonconformist manner, refusing to wear the standard uniform and always expressing his own style.

examples.sentence: During the parade, the drummer seemed to march to the beat of a different drum, setting a unique rhythm that energized the crowd.
examples.translation: During the parade, the drummer acted independently by setting a unique rhythm that energized the crowd.

examples.sentence: She marches to the beat of a different drum when it comes to choosing her career path, often following her own instincts rather than advice.
examples.translation: She acts independently and follows her own inclinations regarding her career path.

examples.sentence: They were told to stay in sync, but he marched to the beat of a different drum, going off on his own during the dance routine.
examples.translation: They were told to act in sync, but he acted independently, going off on his own during the dance routine.

examples.sentence: If you march to the beat of a different drum, you often end up exploring opportunities others overlook.
examples.translation: If you act independently or follow your own inclinations, you often explore opportunities others overlook.

examples.sentence: Mark my words, if you don’t finish that report by Friday, the whole project will fall behind schedule.
examples.translation: Remember what I am saying: if you don’t finish that report by Friday, the whole project will fall behind schedule.

examples.sentence: You can ignore the warnings now, but mark my words, those problems will catch up with you sooner or later.
examples.translation: You can ignore the warnings now, but remember what I am saying: those problems will catch up with you eventually.

examples.sentence: I’m telling you, mark my words, that startup is going to revolutionize the market within the next year.
examples.translation: I’m telling you, remember what I am saying: that startup is going to revolutionize the market within the next year.

examples.sentence: Mark my words, this decision will have consequences that no one is prepared for.
examples.translation: Remember what I am saying, this decision will have consequences that no one is prepared for.

examples.sentence: If you think this is going to be easy, mark my words, you’re in for a surprise.
examples.translation: If you think this will be easy, remember what I just said, you’re in for a surprise.

examples.sentence: Mark my words, the new policy will change everything in the company’s culture.
examples.translation: Remember what I am saying, the new policy will change everything in the company’s culture.

examples.sentence: Mark my words carefully; this advice could save you from making a big mistake.
examples.translation: Remember carefully what I am saying; this advice could save you from making a big mistake.

examples.sentence: She asked him to mark her words so she could remind him later about the promise.
examples.translation: She asked him to remember what she said so she could remind him later about the promise.

examples.sentence: If you mark my words, you’ll prepare thoroughly for the exam and avoid last-minute panic.
examples.translation: If you remember what I said, you’ll prepare thoroughly for the exam and avoid last-minute panic.

examples.sentence: The teacher told the students to mark her words about the importance of punctuality.
examples.translation: The teacher told the students to remember what she said about the importance of punctuality.

examples.sentence: The clerk marked John's card after he completed the registration process.
examples.translation: The clerk recorded information about John on his card after he completed the registration process.

examples.sentence: She was marked on the attendance card as present for the meeting yesterday.
examples.translation: She was recorded on the attendance card as present for the meeting yesterday.

examples.sentence: Every employee must have their card marked after finishing the safety training.
examples.translation: Every employee must have their information recorded after finishing the safety training.

examples.sentence: The system automatically marks the card once the student scans their ID badge.
examples.translation: The system automatically records the student's information once they scan their ID badge.

examples.sentence: After his repeated tardiness, the manager marked his card and warned him about future consequences.
examples.translation: After his repeated tardiness, the manager took note of his behavior for future reference and warned him.

examples.sentence: If you keep slacking off, your card will be marked, and it could affect your promotion chances.
examples.translation: If you keep slacking off, your behavior will be noted and could influence later treatment regarding promotion.

examples.sentence: The supervisor marked her card after noticing her excellent customer service during the busy shift.
examples.translation: The supervisor took note of her excellent customer service for future reference.

examples.sentence: He marked her card when she helped him with the project, promising to return the favor someday.
examples.translation: He mentally registered a favor she did for him, promising to return it someday.

examples.sentence: Don’t forget, I’ve marked your card for that mistake during the presentation.
examples.translation: Don’t forget, I have mentally noted your mistake during the presentation.

examples.sentence: She marked his card when he covered her shift last week, so she’ll help him out when needed.
examples.translation: She mentally registered his help last week and will return the favor when needed.

examples.sentence: Mrs. Thompson was eager to marry off her daughter before the next social season began.
examples.translation: Mrs. Thompson wanted to arrange a marriage for her daughter before the next social season began.

examples.sentence: They decided to marry off their son to a wealthy family to strengthen business ties.
examples.translation: They arranged a marriage for their son to a wealthy family to strengthen business ties.

examples.sentence: The matchmaker worked hard to marry off all her clients by the year's end.
examples.translation: The matchmaker worked hard to arrange marriages for all her clients by the year's end.

examples.sentence: Some parents try to marry off their children quickly just to get them out of the house.
examples.translation: Some parents arrange their children’s marriages to dispose of them quickly, often for convenience.

examples.sentence: He felt his family was trying to marry him off to settle old debts rather than for love.
examples.translation: He felt his family was arranging his marriage to serve their interests rather than his own.

examples.sentence: In some cultures, families marry off daughters early to improve social standing, regardless of their wishes.
examples.translation: In some cultures, families arrange daughters’ marriages for social advantage, often ignoring their wishes.

examples.sentence: The community elders helped marry off several young couples during the festival.
examples.translation: The community elders facilitated the entry of several young couples into marriage during the festival.

examples.sentence: She was happy to marry off her best friend after years of matchmaking efforts.
examples.translation: She was happy to help her best friend enter into marriage after years of matchmaking.

examples.sentence: They had finally married off all the siblings, ensuring the family name continued.
examples.translation: They had finally caused all siblings to enter marriage, ensuring the family name continued.

examples.sentence: The parents worked tirelessly to marry off their daughter to a respected gentleman.
examples.translation: The parents arranged a marriage for their daughter to a respected gentleman.

examples.sentence: It was common in that era to marry off children at a young age to secure alliances.
examples.translation: It was common in that era to arrange marriages for children at a young age to secure alliances.

examples.sentence: They plan to marry off their son by the end of the year once he finishes his studies.
examples.translation: They plan to arrange a marriage for their son by the end of the year after his studies.

examples.sentence: After months of negotiations, they finally married off the young couple in a grand ceremony.
examples.translation: After months of negotiations, they finally arranged the marriage of the young couple in a grand ceremony.

examples.sentence: Whenever I eat my fries, I like to marry the ketchup generously to every bite.
examples.translation: Whenever I eat my fries, I like to combine them with ketchup generously in every bite.

examples.sentence: At the burger joint, he always marries the ketchup with the grilled cheese for an extra flavor punch.
examples.translation: At the burger joint, he always serves the grilled cheese with ketchup for extra flavor.

examples.sentence: She married the ketchup to her scrambled eggs, despite everyone else thinking it was unusual.
examples.translation: She combined ketchup with her scrambled eggs, even though others thought it was unusual.

examples.sentence: The fancy gourmet dish married the ketchup in a way that surprised even the food critics.
examples.translation: The fancy gourmet dish was combined with ketchup in a way that surprised even food critics.

examples.sentence: Some say that adding ketchup to fine steak is like marrying the ketchup to something it doesn’t belong with.
examples.translation: Some say that adding ketchup to fine steak is like combining ketchup with something it doesn’t suit.

examples.sentence: He joked about marrying the ketchup to his high-end meal, blending casual and refined tastes humorously.
examples.translation: He joked about combining ketchup with his high-end meal, blending casual and refined tastes humorously.

examples.sentence: The novel’s plot married the ketchup by mixing a serious theme with slapstick comedy.
examples.translation: The novel’s plot united two dissimilar elements—serious theme and slapstick comedy—in a way that seemed oddly matched.

examples.sentence: Their collaboration married the ketchup, pairing avant-garde art with pop culture references.
examples.translation: Their collaboration united avant-garde art with pop culture references in a way that seemed incongruous or humorous.

examples.sentence: By marrying the ketchup, the chef created a dish that combined traditional flavors with unexpected ingredients.
examples.translation: By combining diverse elements, the chef created a dish that mixed traditional flavors with unexpected ingredients.

examples.sentence: For lunch, I usually marry the ketchup with my burger to enhance the taste.
examples.translation: For lunch, I usually combine ketchup with my burger to enhance the taste.

examples.sentence: He married the ketchup to the fries before serving them to the guests.
examples.translation: He served the fries with ketchup before giving them to the guests.

examples.sentence: They are marrying the ketchup with the hot dogs at the picnic today.
examples.translation: They are serving the hot dogs together with ketchup at the picnic today.

examples.sentence: We will marry the ketchup to the grilled chicken as a dipping sauce.
examples.translation: We will serve grilled chicken with ketchup as a dipping sauce.

examples.sentence: The Mary Celeste was found abandoned in the Atlantic Ocean in 1872 with no sign of the crew.
examples.translation: The Mary Celeste was discovered deserted in the Atlantic Ocean in 1872 without any crew onboard.

examples.sentence: Historians still debate the mysterious circumstances surrounding the Mary Celeste’s disappearance.
examples.translation: Historians continue to discuss the puzzling events related to the Mary Celeste’s disappearance.

examples.sentence: The Mary Celeste remains one of the most famous maritime mysteries of the 19th century.
examples.translation: The Mary Celeste is still a well-known maritime mystery from the 1800s.

examples.sentence: Artifacts recovered from the Mary Celeste have been preserved in maritime museums worldwide.
examples.translation: Objects taken from the Mary Celeste are kept in maritime museums around the world.

examples.sentence: When we returned to the office after the holiday, it looked like a Mary Celeste—completely empty and silent.
examples.translation: When we came back to the office after the holiday, it was completely deserted and silent like the Mary Celeste.

examples.sentence: The abandoned house had a Mary Celeste vibe, with furniture covered in dust and no sign of life.
examples.translation: The abandoned house felt eerie and deserted, similar to the Mary Celeste.

examples.sentence: After the concert, the venue turned into a Mary Celeste, with empty seats and no audience in sight.
examples.translation: After the concert, the venue was deserted and empty like the Mary Celeste.

examples.sentence: The sudden disappearance of the hikers was called a Mary Celeste case by the local news.
examples.translation: The sudden unexplained disappearance of the hikers was referred to as a Mary Celeste case.

examples.sentence: Their office was a Mary Celeste after everyone left abruptly without explanation.
examples.translation: Their office was deserted unexpectedly, like a Mary Celeste situation.

examples.sentence: This abandoned factory has a Mary Celeste mystery about why it was left so suddenly.
examples.translation: This abandoned factory’s sudden desertion is an unexplained mystery similar to the Mary Celeste.

examples.sentence: The earthquake caused mass destruction across the city, leaving thousands homeless.
examples.translation: The earthquake inflicted extensive physical destruction throughout the city, displacing thousands.

examples.sentence: During the war, mass destruction of infrastructure severely hampered relief efforts.
examples.translation: During the war, large-scale destruction of buildings and infrastructure greatly hindered aid.

examples.sentence: Satellite images showed mass destruction in the region after the hurricane hit.
examples.translation: Satellite photos revealed extensive damage and destruction in the area following the hurricane.

examples.sentence: The government allocated funds to rebuild areas affected by mass destruction caused by the floods.
examples.translation: The government provided money to restore areas heavily damaged by widespread destruction from floods.

examples.sentence: The scandal led to mass destruction of the politician’s reputation within weeks.
examples.translation: The scandal caused large-scale ruin of the politician’s reputation in a short time.

examples.sentence: Critics warned that the new policy could bring mass destruction to the company’s brand image.
examples.translation: Critics cautioned that the new policy might cause widespread damage to the company’s reputation.

examples.sentence: The cyber attack caused mass destruction of data, crippling the entire network.
examples.translation: The cyber attack resulted in large-scale annihilation of data, disabling the whole network.

examples.sentence: Master John Goodfellow was the protagonist in the 18th-century folk tale we studied in class.
examples.translation: Master John Goodfellow was the main character in the 18th-century folk story discussed in class.

examples.sentence: The letter was addressed to Master John Goodfellow, indicating his respected status in the village.
examples.translation: The letter was sent to Master John Goodfellow, showing his esteemed position in the community.

examples.sentence: In the novel, Master John Goodfellow is portrayed as a clever and resourceful young man.
examples.translation: In the book, Master John Goodfellow appears as a smart and capable young person.

examples.sentence: Local records mention Master John Goodfellow as a prominent figure in the town's early history.
examples.translation: Historical documents cite Master John Goodfellow as an important person in the town’s early days.

examples.sentence: It was clear that Sarah was the mastermind behind the entire fundraising campaign.
examples.translation: Sarah was the person who conceived, organized, and directed the whole fundraising effort.

examples.sentence: The mastermind of the heist was finally captured after months of investigation.
examples.translation: The chief planner and organizer of the robbery was caught after a long investigation.

examples.sentence: He acted as the mastermind, coordinating every detail of the complex project flawlessly.
examples.translation: He was the main planner and director, managing every aspect of the complicated project perfectly.

examples.sentence: Everyone called Max the mastermind of the group because of his brilliant ideas.
examples.translation: Everyone referred to Max as the smartest and most strategic person in the group due to his clever ideas.

examples.sentence: She joked that she was the mastermind behind their weekend plans, having thought everything through.
examples.translation: She humorously claimed to be the chief thinker who organized their weekend activities.

examples.sentence: You need a mastermind like him to solve this kind of complex problem.
examples.translation: You need a very intelligent and strategic person like him to solve this difficult problem.

examples.sentence: The team asked Julia to mastermind the product launch because of her exceptional organizational skills.
examples.translation: The team asked Julia to plan and direct the product launch, relying on her excellent skills.

examples.sentence: He masterminded the charity event with careful planning and clever resource management.
examples.translation: He skillfully organized and directed the charity event with thoughtful planning.

examples.sentence: They are masterminding a new marketing strategy to boost sales this quarter.
examples.translation: They are planning and directing a new marketing plan to increase sales this quarter.

examples.sentence: The mastermind behind the revolutionary invention was a little-known engineer.
examples.translation: The person who conceived and directed the creation of the revolutionary invention was an obscure engineer.

examples.sentence: Police believe the mastermind of the bank robbery is still at large.
examples.translation: Authorities think the chief planner of the bank robbery has not yet been captured.

examples.sentence: As the mastermind, she organized every step of the complex operation with precision.
examples.translation: In her role as the chief planner, she carefully directed every phase of the complex operation.

examples.sentence: Historians credit the mastermind for the successful campaign that changed the course of history.
examples.translation: Experts recognize the chief organizer responsible for the campaign that altered history.

examples.sentence: In many religions, God is acknowledged as the Master of the Universe, the creator of all things.
examples.translation: In many faiths, God is revered as the supreme deity with absolute authority over everything.

examples.sentence: The concept of a Master of the Universe represents ultimate sovereignty and creative power.
examples.translation: The idea of a Master of the Universe symbolizes complete control and creative authority.

examples.sentence: Ancient texts often describe the Master of the Universe as the source of all life and order.
examples.translation: Old writings frequently portray the Master of the Universe as the origin of existence and harmony.

examples.sentence: Worshipers pray to the Master of the Universe for guidance and protection.
examples.translation: Believers pray to the supreme deity for direction and safety.

examples.sentence: Jake calls himself the Master of the Universe at work because he thinks he controls every decision.
examples.translation: Jake jokingly or arrogantly refers to himself as having total control at work.

examples.sentence: She walked into the meeting like a Master of the Universe, confident and commanding attention.
examples.translation: She entered the meeting with an air of supreme confidence and authority.

examples.sentence: Don’t act like the Master of the Universe just because you got promoted.
examples.translation: Don’t behave arrogantly as if you have supreme power just because you were promoted.

examples.sentence: The villain in the story proclaimed himself Master of the Universe, ruling his dark realm with an iron fist.
examples.translation: The antagonist declared himself supreme ruler of his world, controlling it harshly.

examples.sentence: In sci-fi fiction, the Master of the Universe often wields absolute power over entire galaxies.
examples.translation: In science fiction, the Master of the Universe is a character with total authority over vast cosmic areas.

examples.sentence: The comic book hero battles the Master of the Universe to save his world from destruction.
examples.translation: The superhero fights the supreme villain to protect his world from ruin.

examples.sentence: The company’s master plan involved expanding into new markets and developing innovative products over five years.
examples.translation: The company’s comprehensive strategy included entering new markets and creating new products within five years.

examples.sentence: Before launching the campaign, they laid out a master plan coordinating all departments’ efforts.
examples.translation: Prior to the campaign, they created an overarching plan aligning all teams’ actions.

examples.sentence: Her master plan to improve education combined curriculum reform, teacher training, and community engagement.
examples.translation: Her comprehensive strategy to enhance education involved changing the curriculum, training teachers, and involving the community.
